references.text: [Er50] Erd\H{o}s, P., On integers of the form $2^k+p$ and some related problems. Summa Brasil. Math. (1950), 113-123.

remarks.text:
Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er50} proved that there are infinitely many $n$ such that $f(n)\gg \log\log n$.

Erd\H{o}s could not even prove that there do not exist infinitely many integers $n$ such that for all $1< 2^k<n$ the number $n-2^k$ is prime - see [1142].

The sequence of values of $f(n)$ is A109925 on the OEIS.

See also [237].

statement.text: Let $f(n)$ count the number of solutions to $n=p+2^k$ for prime $p$ and $k\geq 0$. Is it true that $f(n)=o(\log n)$?
